name: Magnesium
contraindications: High supplemental doses can cause GI effects

sources.title: Magnesium Fact Sheet for Health Professionals (NIH ODS)

description: Mg2+ from foods/supplements; major body stores are in bone and soft tissue.
summary: Essential mineral for 300+ enzymatic reactions; supplementation mainly addresses adequacy and intake targets.
mechanism: Cofactor in ATP-linked reactions and ion transport; migraine benefits remain context-dependent.
safetyNotes: Higher supplemental doses can cause diarrhea.
interactions: Can reduce absorption of some antibiotics and bisphosphonates.
dosage: Adult RDA about 310–420 mg/day; supplements often 100–400 mg elemental/day.
preparation: Oxide/citrate/chloride/aspartate salts; food sources include nuts/greens.
regulatoryStatus: Essential nutrient with dietary reference intakes.
